rationale: SOL is the only non-downtrend name on the board, holding its SMA50 (146.1) as support with RSI 40.8 (not extended) and a flat-to-positive MACD, while BTC/ETH/XRP/BNB are all in clear downtrends. Long entry near 147 with a stop just below SMA50 support (144.2, ~1.2x ATR, -1.9%) and a target under the recent high (152.9, +4%) gives ~2:1 reward:risk. Size trimmed to 3% (from the 5% cap) and confidence held to 0.55 because the broad tape is risk-off, which is the main thing that can invalidate this.

transcript.research:
Data source: demo-synthetic (offline). Tape is broadly risk-off on the 1h.
- BTC 75,368: downtrend, price below SMA20 (75,631) and SMA50 (75,991); RSI 45.3, MACD -542, -3.86% 24h, -4.6% from high. Mildly weak, mid-range.
- ETH 3,014.9: downtrend, RSI 33 (near oversold), -6.72% 24h, -15.6% from high. Weakest momentum, deep below SMA50 (3,250).
- SOL 147.10: NEUTRAL, sitting right on SMA50 (146.1), RSI 40.8, MACD slightly +1.2, only -6.2% from high and +13.1% off the low. Best relative strength.
- XRP 0.4651: downtrend, RSI 29.3 (oversold), -4.79% 24h, just 1.8% above the recent low — knife-catch risk.
- BNB 629.2: downtrend, RSI 32.9, -7.39% 24h, hugging its recent low (1.1% above).
Ranked shortlist: (1) SOL long — only name holding structure, not extended. (2) XRP/ETH mean-reversion bounce — oversold but fighting the trend. (3) BNB short on a breakdown of the recent low. Net: one clean-ish long (SOL), the rest are counter-trend gambles.
transcript.bull: Strongest case = LONG SOL/USDT. It's the single name not in a downtrend: price 147.1 is pinned to rising SMA50 support at 146.1, RSI 40.8 leaves room before overbought, and MACD has ticked positive (+1.2) even while the rest of the board bleeds — that relative strength in a red tape is the tell. Entry 147.0, stop 144.2 just under SMA50 and the last swing (~1.2x ATR of 2.39), target 152.9 below the recent high of 156.7. That's 5.9 up vs 2.8 down — about 2.1:1. If the broad tape stabilizes, SOL leads the bounce; you're buying support, not chasing.
transcript.bear: I concede SOL has the best relative strength, but this is a counter-tape long. Four of five symbols are in active downtrends with RSIs in the low 30s; when the leaders (BTC -3.9%, ETH -6.7%) are rolling, an alt-beta long like SOL rarely holds — correlation goes to 1 in selloffs. SOL is only 'neutral' because it's mid-structure; lose 146.1 SMA50 and 144 stop and the next real support is the 130 low, a -12% air pocket. MACD +1.2 is noise, not a trend. The honest read: either stand aside, or if forced, the higher-probability trade is shorting BNB on a break of its 622.7 low. At minimum, size must be small.
transcript.risk:
Config: max size 5% equity, leverage 3x, stop 2%, TP 4%, max drawdown 20%. No open positions / API not queried in this demo, so exposure starts flat.
Verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. The setup is acceptable but the bear's correlation point is valid in a broadly red tape, so I will not allow full size. Stop at 144.2 is -1.9% (within the 2% rule) and ~1.2x ATR — wide enough to survive normal noise, tight enough to cap loss. At 3% size and 3x leverage the notional is ~9% of equity; a stop-out costs ~0.057% of equity — comfortably inside drawdown limits. Max size I will allow: 3% (down from 5%). Keep leverage at 3x. No stacking another alt long until this resolves.
transcript.portfolio: Bull and bear are close; SOL's relative strength and the clean 2:1 to support tip it to a small long, but the risk-off tape and the Risk Manager's correlation veto-pressure cap size at 3% and confidence at 0.55.
